# Day 6 – Resume Optimization

## Objective

Learn how AI can generate and optimize resumes for ATS (Applicant Tracking Systems).

---

## My Experiment

Unlike previous tasks, I did not have an existing resume.

Instead of optimizing a resume, Claude first collected information about:

* Education
* Skills
* Experience
* Projects
* Certifications
* Career Goals

Using this information, it generated a one-page professional resume and performed an ATS analysis.

---

## Resume Generated By Claude

![Generated Resume](Prateek_Kumar_Singh_Resume.pdf)

---

## ATS Analysis

**ATS Score: 74/100**

The resume performed reasonably well for an entry-level student profile.

The analysis highlighted strengths such as:

* Clear structure
* Relevant technical skills
* Project section
* Professional summary
* Industry-relevant keywords

---

## Observations

* Claude was able to create a complete resume from a simple question-and-answer process.
* The generated resume was organized and ATS-friendly.
* My Content Creation and Social Media experience was translated into professional resume language.
* Technical skills were categorized clearly, making the resume easier to scan.

---

## What Could Be Improved?

* Add stronger Data Analytics projects.
* Include measurable achievements where possible.
* Earn more industry-recognized certifications.
* Add internship or practical experience in Data Analytics or Data Science.

These additions would likely improve both ATS performance and recruiter appeal.

---

## Key Learning

I learned that AI can do more than edit documents.

By collecting context first, it can generate professional documents from scratch and adapt them to industry standards.

The quality of the final resume depended heavily on the quality of the information provided.

---

## Conclusion

This experiment showed me how AI can simplify resume creation and optimization.

Even without an existing resume, Claude was able to generate a structured one-page resume and evaluate it using ATS principles.

The biggest takeaway was that AI is most effective when it combines information gathering, document generation, and feedback into a single workflow.
